_README.purpose: Translations for worksheet 04 (Electrical Kitchen Appliances & Conservation of Energy).
_README.language: English — reference file. Original content authored by the Turkish partner.
_README.instructions: All other languages copy this file and translate the values. Keys (left of the colon) must never change. See translator-guide.html for full instructions.

meta.title: Electrical Kitchen Appliances & Conservation of Energy
meta.topic: Conservation of energy in electrical kitchen appliances — oven, refrigerator and dishwasher.

learningOutcomes._comment: Four bullet points in the structural table.
learningOutcomes.lo1: State the Law of Conservation of Energy and identify the dominant energy transformation in each appliance.
learningOutcomes.lo2: Use the formulas P = V × I and E = P × t to calculate power and energy.
learningOutcomes.lo3: Distinguish resistive loads (cos φ = 1) from motor/compressor loads (cos φ < 1).
learningOutcomes.lo4: Calculate the cost of running an appliance for a given time at a given electricity tariff.

structural._comment: Other rows of the structural table.
structural.resources: Calculator, electricity tariff (e.g. 0.30 €/kWh), appliance nameplate data (or manufacturer website).

electricalTheory._comment: Page 1 — three sub-sections: Conservation of Energy, Basic Electrical Concepts and Power in AC Circuits.
electricalTheory.consBody: The Law of Conservation of Energy states that energy cannot be created or destroyed, only transformed. In kitchen appliances:
electricalTheory.acB2: cos φ < 1 → motors and compressors (refrigerator, dishwasher)

oven._comment: Section 1 of the appliances — the electric oven (pages 2–3).
oven.title: 1 — Electric Oven
oven.howBody: An electric oven converts electrical energy into heat using heating elements.
oven.energyFormula: The electricity consumption is calculated with:
oven.calcTitle: Worked example — 2.5 kW oven for 1 hour
oven.resultBody: A 2.5 kW oven running for 1 hour costs approximately 0.75 € (75 euro cents) in electricity.

ovenTest._comment: Quiz questions 1–5. The 'a/b/c/d' fields are the four options in order; the correct one is marked in the Answer Key.
ovenTest.title: Oven test
ovenTest.q1text: What effect produces heat in the oven?
ovenTest.q2text: Main energy conversion in an oven:
ovenTest.q5text: cos φ in a heating element is:

fridge.title: 2 — Refrigerator
fridge.howBody: A refrigerator removes heat from inside and releases it outside.
fridge.howB2: Heat is absorbed inside the cabinet.
fridge.howB3: Heat is released outside (through the back coils).

fridgeTest.title: Refrigerator test
fridgeTest.q6text: The main function of a refrigerator is to:
fridgeTest.q9text: cos φ of a refrigerator compressor is:

dish.title: 3 — Dishwasher
dish.howBody: A dishwasher cleans dishes using hot water and mechanical action.
dish.physB3: A combination of thermal + mechanical energy.
dish.calcTitle: Worked example — 1.5 kW dishwasher for 1 hour
dish.resultBody: A 1.5 kW dishwasher running for 1 hour costs approximately 0.45 € (45 euro cents) in electricity.

dishTest.title: Dishwasher test
dishTest.q11text: What heats the water in a dishwasher?
dishTest.q12text: What moves the water around inside?
dishTest.q14text: cos φ of a dishwasher (motor + heater) is:
